**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

Request email verification code (OTP).

**Notes:**
- OTP codes expire after 10 minutes
- Rate limited to 1 request per 30 seconds per email/device
- If authenticated non-anonymous user requests OTP for an email already registered to another account, returns 409 Conflict
- Admin accounts (staff/superuser) always receive real OTP even in mock mode for security


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Verify email and authenticate/register.

**Authentication Flows:**
- **Unauthenticated user + new email** → REGISTERED (new account created)
- **Unauthenticated user + existing email** → LOGGED_IN (login to existing account)
- **Anonymous user + new email** → REGISTERED (anonymous completes registration)
- **Anonymous user + existing email** → MERGED (anonymous merged into existing account)
- **Authenticated user + own/new email** → MODIFIED (user adds/changes email)
- **Invalid/expired code** → REJECTED

**Status values:**
- `REJECTED` - Invalid code, expired code, or blocked
- `REGISTERED` - New account created or anonymous completed registration
- `LOGGED_IN` - Existing user logged in
- `MERGED` - Anonymous user merged into existing account
- `MODIFIED` - Authenticated user added/changed email


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Overridable serializer seams for stapel-auth API views.

Views declare ``<purpose>_serializer_class`` class attributes following the
``*_request_serializer_class`` / ``*_response_serializer_class`` naming
convention (e.g. ``request_serializer_class`` or, when a view uses several
serializers, purpose-prefixed names such as ``login_request_serializer_class``
or ``auth_response_serializer_class``). For every such attribute this mixin
supplies the matching ``get_<purpose>_serializer_class()`` getter, so hosts
can swap a serializer by subclassing the view and overriding either the
attribute or the getter::

    class MyMagicLinkViewSet(MagicLinkViewSet):
        response_serializer_class = MyResponseSerializer

Handler bodies instantiate serializers exclusively through the getters, so
an override is picked up everywhere the serializer is used.

**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Request phone verification code (SMS OTP).

**Notes:**
- OTP codes expire after 10 minutes
- Rate limited to 1 request per 30 seconds per phone/device
- If authenticated non-anonymous user requests OTP for a phone already registered to another account, returns 409 Conflict
- Admin accounts (staff/superuser) always receive real OTP even in mock mode for security


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Verify phone number and authenticate/register.

**Authentication Flows:**
- **Unauthenticated user + new phone** → REGISTERED (new account created)
- **Unauthenticated user + existing phone** → LOGGED_IN (login to existing account)
- **Anonymous user + new phone** → REGISTERED (anonymous completes registration)
- **Anonymous user + existing phone** → MERGED (anonymous merged into existing account)
- **Authenticated user + own/new phone** → MODIFIED (user adds/changes phone)
- **Invalid/expired code** → REJECTED

**Status values:**
- `REJECTED` - Invalid code, expired code, or blocked
- `REGISTERED` - New account created or anonymous completed registration
- `LOGGED_IN` - Existing user logged in
- `MERGED` - Anonymous user merged into existing account
- `MODIFIED` - Authenticated user added/changed phone


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Confirm a `login_request` QR code (called by the logged-in scanner after reviewing).

Issues tokens for the waiting device. The device polling `/status` will receive the tokens.


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Reject a QR auth request. The device polling `/status` will receive `rejected`.

**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

get.description:
Browser endpoint embedded in QR code. Processes the scan and redirects.

**session_share** (scanner has no session) → logs scanner in as the QR owner and redirects to `redirect_url`.
**session_share** (scanner already logged in as the same user) → marks fulfilled, redirects.
**session_share** (scanner logged in as a *different* user) → redirects with `?qr_status=account_conflict`.
**login_request** (scanner logged in) → redirects to `/qr-confirm?key=…` for confirmation.
**login_request** (scanner not logged in) → redirects to `/sign-in?redirect=<scan_url>`.


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Poll the status of a QR auth key.

**Statuses:**
- `pending` — waiting for scan/confirm.
- `fulfilled` — action completed; for `login_request`, tokens are included so the polling device can authenticate.
- `expired` — key was not found (TTL elapsed).
- `rejected` — scanner or confirmer rejected the request; show error UI.


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

post.description:
Generate a short-lived QR auth key (5 min TTL).

**Types:**
- `session_share` — a logged-in device generates a QR; when another device scans it, that device receives the same session. Requires authentication.
- `login_request` — an unauthenticated device generates a QR and polls for approval; a logged-in scanner confirms the login.

**`redirect_url`** (optional) — where to send the scanner after the auth flow completes. Defaults to `/`.

**Response:** encode `scan_url` into a QR image (e.g. via qrcode.js) and display it. Poll `GET /qr/{key}/status/` to know when the flow completes.


**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

Backend-facing endpoint the IdP POSTs to after authentication. The body is an external, application/x-www-form-urlencoded form carrying a base64-encoded `SAMLResponse` (and optional `RelayState`) — its schema is defined by the SAML 2.0 spec, not this API. Always responds with a 302 redirect to the frontend (with session cookies on success, or an `?error=` query param on failure).

DEPRECATED (removed in 1.0). Issue a one-time step-up token after TOTP verification (valid 15 min, X-Step-Up-Token). Superseded by the unified step-up contract: guard sensitive actions with @requires_verification and drive the /verification/ envelope flow instead. For transit, a successful call ALSO writes a server-side verification grant for the LEGACY_STEP_UP_GRANT_SCOPES scopes, so deployed legacy frontends keep passing @requires_verification while the backend migrates. See auth-stepup-unification.md.

**Permissions:** `IsServiceRequest, IsSuperUser`

Base view exposing serializer seams.

Every concrete view declares ``request_serializer_class`` /
``response_serializer_class`` (``None`` when that direction carries no
serialized payload). Subclasses may swap either class attribute — or
override the getters — to customize the request/response envelopes
without rewriting the method bodies.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

serialized payload). Subclasses may swap either class attribute — or
override the getters — to customize the request/response envelopes
without rewriting the method bodies.

get.description:
Base view exposing serializer seams.

Every concrete view declares ``request_serializer_class`` /
``response_serializer_class`` (``None`` when that direction carries no
serialized payload). Subclasses may swap either class attribute — or
override the getters — to customize the request/response envelopes
without rewriting the method bodies.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

Step-up verification challenge info. Returns the challenge's scope and its factor list filtered to the factors this user can actually complete — build the factor picker UI from it. 404 for a missing, expired or another user's challenge.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

post.description:
Complete the challenge with the factor's proof (code / backup_code / passkey assertion). On success the server-side grant for the challenge's scope is written and a stateless verification_token is returned — retry the original request (optionally with the X-Verification-Token header). On failure: 400 while attempts remain, 423 once the challenge is invalidated by too many failures.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

Initiate a verification factor for the challenge: sends the one-time code (otp_email/otp_phone) or produces WebAuthn request options (passkey; totp has nothing to initiate). Returns factor-specific client data — masked destination or the WebAuthn options with the ceremony session_key. The factor must be in the challenge's list and available to the user.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

get.description:
The current user's step-up verification preferences: one row per scope the user has touched (enabled=False turns a default_on scope off, enabled=True turns an opt_in scope on). Scopes without a row follow the endpoint's level default; strict scopes ignore preferences entirely.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

put.description:
Upsert a step-up verification preference {scope, enabled}. Enabling (enabled=true) applies immediately. Disabling (enabled=false) is itself step-up protected: without a fresh grant for scope verification.settings the request is rejected with the 403 verification envelope — complete a factor and retry. Both writes invalidate the cached policy, so protected endpoints see the change within one request.

**Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`

post.description:
Verify JWT token

**Permissions:** `AllowAny`

AuthResponse.description: Authentication response with user data and tokens.

status.description:
Authentication outcome

* `REJECTED` - Invalid code, expired code, or validation error
* `REGISTERED` - New account created or anonymous completed registration
* `LOGGED_IN` - Existing user logged in
* `MERGED` - Anonymous user merged into existing account
* `MODIFIED` - Authenticated user added or changed email/phone

AuthResponseStatusEnum.description:
* `REJECTED` - Invalid code, expired code, or validation error
* `REGISTERED` - New account created or anonymous completed registration
* `LOGGED_IN` - Existing user logged in
* `MERGED` - Anonymous user merged into existing account
* `MODIFIED` - Authenticated user added or changed email/phone

ClosureStatusDTO.description: Status of an account closure request.

status.description: One of grace, deleting, deleted, cancelled

grace_ends_at.description: ISO datetime when grace period ends

can_cancel.description: Whether the closure can still be cancelled

DelayedChangeCancel.description: Cancel a pending delayed change request.

scheduled_at.description: ISO 8601 date when the change will be applied

can_cancel_until.description: ISO 8601 deadline to cancel the change

has_pending_change.description: Whether there is a pending change

change_request_id.description: UUID of the pending change request

days_remaining.description: Days until the change is applied

notifications_sent.description: List of notification types already sent

ExportRequestDTO.description: Response after initiating a data export request.

message.example: Your archive will be ready within 48 hours.

ExportStatusDTO.description: Status of a data export request.

status.description: One of pending, processing, ready, failed, expired

download_available.description: Whether archive is ready to download

expires_at.description: ISO datetime when download link expires, null if not ready

token.description: Single-use download token bound to the authenticated user.

InstantChangeRequestNew.description: Request OTP to new authenticator (phone or email).

InstantChangeVerifyNew.description: Verify OTP from new authenticator and apply change.

message.example: Verification code sent to new address

InstantRequestOldResponse.description: OTP sent to current authenticator for instant change.

InstantVerifyOldResponse.description: Current authenticator verified, change token issued.

change_token.description: Token to authorize the change, pass to verify-new

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiration time for the change token

refresh_token.description: Refresh token to blacklist (optional, will also use cookie if available)

MagicLinkRequestBody.description:
DRF serializer mixin that validates a ``captcha_token`` field.

Add this mixin **before** ``serializers.Serializer`` in the MRO so that
``validate_captcha_token`` runs during DRF field-level validation.

Call ``self._require_captcha_if_configured(attrs)`` from ``validate()``
to return ``ERR_400_CAPTCHA_REQUIRED`` when captcha is active but the
client omitted the token entirely.

redirect_url.description: Relative path to land on after login, e.g. /app or /meeting/abc. Must start with /. Absolute URLs are rejected.

OtpSentResponse.description: OTP has been sent to the target.

target.description: Masked email or phone where OTP was sent

PasswordMethod.description: A single available password change / reset method.

method.description:
Method identifier

* `password` - Change via the current (old) password
* `email` - Change via OTP sent to the verified email
* `phone` - Change via OTP sent to the verified phone
* `totp` - Change via TOTP authenticator app code

PasswordMethodMethodEnum.description:
* `password` - Change via the current (old) password
* `email` - Change via OTP sent to the verified email
* `phone` - Change via OTP sent to the verified phone
* `totp` - Change via TOTP authenticator app code

PasswordMethodsResponse.description: Available methods for changing or resetting the password.

has_password.description: Whether the account already has a password set

PasswordRegister.description:
DRF serializer mixin that validates a ``captcha_token`` field.

Add this mixin **before** ``serializers.Serializer`` in the MRO so that
``validate_captcha_token`` runs during DRF field-level validation.

Call ``self._require_captcha_if_configured(attrs)`` from ``validate()``
to return ``ERR_400_CAPTCHA_REQUIRED`` when captcha is active but the
client omitted the token entirely.

PasswordResetEmailRequest.description:
DRF serializer mixin that validates a ``captcha_token`` field.

Add this mixin **before** ``serializers.Serializer`` in the MRO so that
``validate_captcha_token`` runs during DRF field-level validation.

Call ``self._require_captcha_if_configured(attrs)`` from ``validate()``
to return ``ERR_400_CAPTCHA_REQUIRED`` when captcha is active but the
client omitted the token entirely.

PasswordResetPhoneRequest.description:
DRF serializer mixin that validates a ``captcha_token`` field.

Add this mixin **before** ``serializers.Serializer`` in the MRO so that
``validate_captcha_token`` runs during DRF field-level validation.

Call ``self._require_captcha_if_configured(attrs)`` from ``validate()``
to return ``ERR_400_CAPTCHA_REQUIRED`` when captcha is active but the
client omitted the token entirely.

domain.description: Email domain tied to this org, e.g. acmecorp.com

sso_enforced.description: If true, members must log in via SSO only

saml_x509_cert.description: IdP signing certificate (PEM or raw base64)

type.description:
`session_share` — logged-in user generates a QR to share their session with a scanner (e.g. log into a new device). Requires auth. `login_request` — unauthenticated device generates a QR and waits for a logged-in scanner to approve the login.

* `session_share` - session_share
* `login_request` - login_request

redirect_url.description: Where to redirect the scanner after successful auth. Must be a relative path starting with / (e.g. /home). For `session_share`: the scanning device lands here after receiving the session. For `login_request`: the confirming device lands here after approving. Defaults to `/` if omitted.

allow_unauthenticated_scanner.description: `session_share` only: explicitly allow a scanner with no session to receive the owner's session. Default false — an unauthenticated scan of a session_share QR is rejected with 403.

type.description:
QR type

* `session_share` - Logged-in user shares their session to another device
* `login_request` - Unauthenticated device requests approval from a logged-in scanner

scan_url.description: The URL to encode inside the QR image. When a phone camera scans the QR code it opens this URL on the scanner's device, which triggers the auth flow. Pass this to your QR-code renderer (e.g. qrcode.js)

QRGenerateResponseTypeEnum.description:
* `session_share` - Logged-in user shares their session to another device
* `login_request` - Unauthenticated device requests approval from a logged-in scanner

QRStatusResponse.description: Current status of a QR auth key.

status.description:
Key lifecycle status

* `pending` - Waiting for scan or confirm
* `fulfilled` - Action completed; tokens available for login_request
* `expired` - Key not found (TTL elapsed)
* `rejected` - Scanner or confirmer explicitly rejected the request

QRStatusResponseStatusEnum.description:
* `pending` - Waiting for scan or confirm
* `fulfilled` - Action completed; tokens available for login_request
* `expired` - Key not found (TTL elapsed)
* `rejected` - Scanner or confirmer explicitly rejected the request

SSODomainLookupResponse.description: SSO organization lookup result for a given email domain.

sso_required.description: Whether SSO is enforced for this domain

org_slug.description: Organization slug to use in the SSO login URL

protocol.description: SSO protocol in use (saml or oidc)

SecurityStatusContact.description: Masked contact (email or phone) with verification state.

value.description: Masked contact value, null if not set

connected_providers.description: List of connected OAuth provider IDs

is_set.description: Whether a password is set on the account

SecurityStatusResponse.description: Full security posture for the current user.

backup_codes_remaining.description: How many unused backup codes are left

is_current.description: Whether this is the caller's current session

is_suspicious.description: Whether login was flagged as suspicious

status.description: Short status key describing the completed action

StaffRoleAssignRequest.description:
Assign a staff role: target user UUID + a role name from the
STAPEL_ACCESS["ROLES"] registry (validated in the service layer).

StaffRoleAssignment.description: One user → role assignment row (read-only representation).

StapelError.description: Structured error returned by all Stapel API endpoints.

TOTPChallengeResponse.description: Intermediate response when TOTP 2FA is required to complete login.

status.description:
Always TOTP_REQUIRED

* `TOTP_REQUIRED` - Login succeeded but TOTP verification is needed to complete it

TOTPChallengeResponseStatusEnum.description: * `TOTP_REQUIRED` - Login succeeded but TOTP verification is needed to complete it

TOTPSetupConfirmResponse.description: Result of confirming TOTP setup. Store backup codes securely.

backup_codes.description: One-time backup codes. Each usable once if authenticator is lost

TOTPSetupResponse.description: TOTP enrollment data — pass to authenticator app.

secret.description: Base32 TOTP secret for manual entry

qr_uri.description: otpauth URI suitable for QR code display

TOTPStepUpResponse.description: Issued step-up token after TOTP verification.

step_up_token.description: Opaque token proving recent TOTP verification

expires_in.description: Seconds until the step-up token expires

token.description: The JWT access token to introspect.

TokenIntrospectResponse.description:
RFC 7662 introspection response.

``active`` is always present; the claim fields are only populated when the
token is valid.

active.description: Whether the token is currently valid.

username.description: Required. 150 characters or fewer. Letters, digits and @/./+/-/_ only.

is_staff.title: Staff status
is_staff.description: Designates whether the user can log into this admin site.

is_superuser.title: Superuser status
is_superuser.description: Designates that this user has all permissions without explicitly assigning them.

VerificationChallengeInfoResponse.description: Step-up verification challenge as seen by its owner.

scope.description: Scope of the protected action the challenge guards

factors.description: Interchangeable factors this user can actually complete

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp when the challenge expires

VerificationComplete.description:
Request body for completing a verification challenge.

``factor`` selects the factor; the remaining fields are the factor's
proof payload — ``code`` / ``backup_code`` for OTP/TOTP factors,
``session_key`` + ``credential`` for the passkey assertion.

factor.description: Factor id from the challenge's factor list, e.g. otp_email.

session_key.description: WebAuthn ceremony key returned by initiate (passkey).

VerificationCompleteResponse.description: Successful completion of a verification challenge.

verification_token.description: Stateless proof of verification — send as X-Verification-Token when retrying the original request (the grant is also stored server-side)

VerificationInitiate.description: Request body for initiating a verification factor.

factor.description: Factor id from the challenge's factor list, e.g. otp_email.

VerificationInitiateResponse.description: Result of initiating a verification factor.

data.description: Factor-specific client data — masked destination for OTP factors, WebAuthn request options (with session_key) for passkey

VerificationPreference.description: Request body for upserting a verification preference.

scope.description: Verification scope the preference applies to, e.g. wallet.withdraw.

enabled.description: True enables step-up for the scope (opt_in endpoints), False disables it (default_on endpoints). Disabling requires a fresh step-up grant for scope verification.settings.

VerificationPreferenceRow.description: One step-up verification preference of the current user.

scope.description: Verification scope the preference applies to

enabled.description: Whether step-up is enabled for this scope — False turns a default_on scope off, True turns an opt_in scope on

VerificationPreferencesResponse.description: The current user's step-up verification preferences.

preferences.description: Preference rows, one per scope the user has touched — scopes without a row follow the endpoint's level default

domain.description: Email domain tied to this org, e.g. acmecorp.com

sso_enforced.description: If true, members must log in via SSO only

saml_x509_cert.description: IdP signing certificate (PEM or raw base64)

JWTCookieAuth.name: stapel_jwt
JWTCookieAuth.description: JWT token stored in cookie. Login via /auth/admin/ to get the cookie.
